title: Understanding Angular Acceleration
text: Angular acceleration is the rate of change of angular velocity with respect to time. It is given by the derivative of angular velocity with respect to time.

title: Derivative of Angular Velocity
text: The angular velocity is given by $$omega=25t$$. To find the angular acceleration, take the derivative of omega with respect to $$t$$.

title: Calculate the Derivative
text: What is the derivative of $$omega=25t$$ with respect to $$t$$?

title: Constant Angular Acceleration
text: Since the derivative of $$omega=25t$$ is a constant, the angular acceleration is constant over time.
